response:
[SOFA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[THE TIME ALLOCATED PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY IS 45 MINUTES DIVIDED BY 25 QUESTIONS, EQUAL TO 1.8 MINUTES PER QUESTION, OR 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. FOR CHEMISTRY, 1 HOUR IS 60 MINUTES, DIVIDED BY 20 QUESTIONS, EQUAL TO 3 MINUTES PER QUESTION, OR 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. THE DIFFERENCE IS 180 MINUS 108 EQUALS 72 SECONDS. SHE CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN CHEMISTRY COMPARED TO BIOLOGY.]

[THE CHEMISTRY TEST REQUIREMENTS ARE MORE TIME PER QUESTION, SO SHE HAS MORE SECONDS TO SPEND ON EACH QUESTION. THIS MEANS SHE CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN CHEMISTRY COMPARED TO BIOLOGY. THE TIME ALLOCATED PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY IS 3 MINUTES, OR 180 SECONDS, WHILE FOR BIOLOGY IT IS 1.8 MINUTES, OR 108 SECONDS. THE DIFFERENCE IS 72 SECONDS.]

[THE CHEMISTRY TEST REQUIREMENTS ARE MORE TIME PER QUESTION, SO SHE HAS MORE SECONDS TO SPEND ON EACH QUESTION. THIS MEANS SHE CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN CHEMISTRY COMPARED TO BIOLOGY. THE TIME ALLOCATED PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY IS 3 MINUTES, OR 180 SECONDS, WHILE FOR BIOLOGY IT IS 1.8 MINUTES, OR 108 SECONDS. THE DIFFERENCE IS 72 SECONDS.]
